_note: Prose-only, token-less — the subtractive economy pass. Rubric from fixtures/prune-tests/expected-findings.md against cart.spec.js: a redundant pair (same behavior contract, same example), one over-mock of an internal collaborator, one stale test (name/comment claim a $X.XX string, assertion checks typeof number), and one genuine keeper (empty-cart boundary). prune-tests PROPOSES a categorized plan (merge/rewrite/rename/keep) and deletes nothing. The hollow sample is the under-reach failure — 'suite is lean, keep all' — which misses the real debt. `anchors` exist only for the offline heuristic judge; the LLM judge grades `claim` directly.

must_surface.claim: Proposes consolidating the redundant pair `adds two items` + `sums item prices` — identical behavior contract tested through the same example (same expected-use scenario class) → merge.

Flags the over-mocking in `applies tax` — it mocks the internal `tax` collaborator and only asserts `tax.rate()` was called, so it verifies the mock rather than the `withTax` math → rewrite with a real collaborator.

Flags `returns cents as a formatted string` as out-of-sync / stale — the name and Given/When/Then comment claim a `"$X.XX"` string contract, but the assertion only checks `typeof === 'number'`; name/intent no longer match the behavior → rename/rewrite.

must_not.claim: Must NOT claim any test is confirmed false-confidence — that requires a mutation and is `/audit-test`'s job; a suspected false-confidence test is deferred, not judged here (expected-findings boundary).
Must NOT propose NEW tests for missing paths — adding coverage is `/coverage-review`'s additive job, not this subtractive one.
